problems.title: AWK: Print the second column
problems.instructions:
Given input with at least two whitespace-separated fields per line, print only the **second field** of each line.

Example input:

a b c
d e f

Expected output:

b
e

{hints: Use $2 to reference the second field; remember that AWK splits on whitespace by default.}

tests.input:
a b c
d e f

problems.title: AWK: Sum numbers in the first column
problems.instructions:
Read numbers from the **first column** and print the sum of all those numbers as a single line.

Example input:

1
2
3

Output:

6

{hints: Use a variable (e.g. sum) that you update for each line and print it in the END block.}

problems.title: AWK: Filter lines containing a word
problems.instructions:
Print only the lines that contain the word apple anywhere in the line.

Input:

apple
banana
apple pie

Output:

apple
apple pie

{hints: You can use a pattern like /apple/ { print } to match lines containing that substring.}

problems.title: AWK: Count how many lines
problems.instructions:
Count the total number of input lines and print that number once at the end.

{hints: Increment a counter for every record (NR or a custom variable) and print it in END.}

problems.title: AWK: Print the last column
problems.instructions:
For each input line, print only the **last field**.

Example:

1 2 3
4 5 6

Output:

3
6

{hints: NF holds the number of fields; $NF refers to the last field.}

problems.title: AWK: Replace foo with bar
problems.instructions:
For every line, replace all occurrences of foo with bar and print the modified line.

Input:

foo
foobar
baz

Output:

bar
barbar
baz

{hints: Use gsub("foo", "bar") to replace all matches in $0, then print the line.}

problems.title: AWK: First and last columns
problems.instructions:
Print only the **first** and **last** fields from each line, separated by a single space.

Example:

a b c d
e f g h

Output:

a d
e h

{hints: Combine $1 and $NF inside a single print statement.}

tests.input:
a b c d
e f g h

problems.title: AWK: Lines longer than 5 characters
problems.instructions:
Print only the lines whose total length (number of characters) is strictly greater than 5.

Input:

hi
hello
welcome

Output:

welcome

{hints: length($0) returns the length of the entire line; you can use it in a condition before print.}

problems.title: AWK: Unique values in first column
problems.instructions:
Given a list of values in the first column, print each unique value **once**, in the order of first appearance.

Input:

a
b
a
c
b

Output:

a
b
c

{hints: Use an associative array to remember which values you have already printed.}

problems.title: AWK: Prefix lines with numbers
problems.instructions:
Print each line prefixed with its line number and a space.

Input:

foo
bar
baz

Output:

1 foo
2 bar
3 baz

{hints: NR is the current record (line) number; use it directly in your print statement.}
